heading.purpose: Group name at the top of the group page only.

body.purpose: Default size for nearly everything: labels, list items, buttons.

motion.name: spinner
motion.purpose: Submit-button pending indicator only; no other motion in the system.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action across forms (Add expense, Save changes, Create group).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #18181b; color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 20px; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #3f3f46; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #71717a; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Outline button for secondary actions (Settle up, History, Cancel).
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffffff; color: #3f3f46; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #d4d4d8; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #f4f4f5; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #71717a; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Expense Card
components.description: Solid-border card for an expense row in the activity feed.
components.html:
Dinner at the night market45.00 SGD
9:42 pm · Food & Drink
components.css: .ds-card { display: block; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; background: #ffffff; text-decoration: none; } .ds-card-solid { border: 1px solid #e4e4e7; } .ds-card-solid:hover { border-color: #d4d4d8; } .ds-card-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #18181b; } .ds-card-amount { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #3f3f46; } .ds-card-meta { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #71717a; margin-top: 4px; }

components.name: Settlement Card
components.description: Dashed-border card — the one reserved visual signal that money moved between people, not an expense.
components.html:
Alice → Bob29.90 SGD
Settlement

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text/number/date field used across every form.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" placeholder="e.g. Dinner at the night market" />
components.css: .ds-input { border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #d4d4d8; background: #ffffff; padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #18181b; outline: none; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #71717a; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #a1a1aa; }

components.name: New Badge
components.description: Marks an activity-feed item created since this device's last visit — the only filled-background use of a semantic color in the system.
components.css: .ds-badge-new { display: inline-block; background: #d1fae5; color: #047857; font-size: 0.625rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 4px; }

narrative.overview: Splitto looks like a plain paper ledger passed between friends, not a finance dashboard trying to earn your trust with polish. There is no signature color, no gradient, no shadow anywhere in the interface — every screen is built from Tailwind's default neutral (zinc) scale, plain borders, and a small, deliberate set of semantic colors used only where they carry real meaning. The system explicitly rejects Splitwise's busier, ad-supported feel: no toolbars, no upsell banners, no visual competition for attention against the actual task of logging an expense or settling up. Density is low and whitespace is generous. Cards are flat rectangles with a hairline border, nothing more — depth is never implied with a shadow, only with a change in background tone. The one structural flourish the system allows itself is a dashed border, reserved specifically for settlement records.
keyCharacteristics: Zinc neutral scale as the entire base palette; color is spent only on meaning, never on decoration
Flat by construction — no box-shadow anywhere in the codebase
Solid border = expense, dashed border = settlement; the one recurring structural signal
Generous whitespace, small type scale, no dense data-table aesthetic despite being a money app

rules.name: The One Line Rule
rules.body: Depth is conveyed with exactly one hairline border between a card and the page behind it — never a shadow, never a second border, never a gradient edge.

rules.name: The Dash Means Money Moved Rule
rules.body: A dashed border is the single reserved signal for a settlement. Every other card uses a solid border.

rules.name: The One Size Mostly Rule
rules.body: The interface runs almost entirely on two sizes — 0.875rem for content, 0.75rem for metadata.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No component in this system ever casts a shadow. If a new component seems to need one to feel grounded, the fix is a border or a background-tone shift.

dos: Do keep color spent only on meaning: green for owed-to-you, red for owed-by-you, amber for an estimate or a second look
Do use the dashed border exclusively for settlements; every other card stays solid
Do convey depth with a single hairline border and a background-tone step, never a shadow
Do show a spinner and disable a submit button the instant its action starts
donts: Don't add Splitwise's busier, ad-supported feel — no toolbars, no upsell banners
Don't introduce a shadow anywhere; this system has none by design
Don't invent a second accent color; the palette is neutral-plus-three-semantic-colors
Don't use the dashed-border treatment for anything other than a settlement record
